content.problemStatement:
You are given two integers NNN and KKK, and an array AAA of NNN integers. You have to choose KKK disjoint non-empty subarrays such that the score is maximized.

The score is calculated as follows:

Score=∑i=1KSum[i]⋅i,\mathrm{Score}= \sum_{i=1}^{K} \mathrm{Sum}[i] \cdot i,Score=i=1∑K​Sum[i]⋅i,where Sum[i]\mathrm{Sum}[i]Sum[i] denotes sum of elements of iii-th subarray. By the iii-th subarray, we mean the iii-th one in the order from left to right.

Find the maximum score that can be achieved.

**Note:** The subarrays are not required to cover the whole array. It is allowed for some elements of AAA to belong to none of the subarrays.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\dots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the maximum score.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: One way to choose 222 disjoint non-empty subarrays is:

S1=[1,2]S_1 = [1,2]S1​=[1,2] and S2=[3,1]S_2 = [3,1]S2​=[3,1]
The score is 3⋅1+4⋅2=113\cdot 1 + 4\cdot 2 =113⋅1+4⋅2=11, which is the maximum possible.

Test Case 2: One way to choose 222 disjoint non-empty subarrays is:

S1=[2,11]S_1 = [2,11]S1​=[2,11] and S2=[12]S_2 = [12]S2​=[12]
The score is 13⋅1+12⋅2=3713\cdot 1+12\cdot 2 = 3713⋅1+12⋅2=37, which is the maximum possible.
